description: Evaluates the 'Before You Start (Agent Bootstrap)' section behavior

evals.name: happy-path-server-running
evals.prompt: I want to launch an 8xH100 cluster for training a LLaMA model. My SkyPilot is already set up.
evals.expected_output: Agent runs `sky api status -l 1`, sees the server is running (table output with request data), skips rest of bootstrap (no `sky check`, no install, no `sky api start`), and proceeds directly to help write the task YAML or run `sky launch`.

assertions.text: Agent runs `sky api status` (or `sky api status -l 1`) as the first SkyPilot command
Agent does NOT run `sky check` when the server is already running
Agent does NOT run `sky api start` or `sky api login` when the server is already running
Agent proceeds to help with the user's actual task (launching H100 cluster) without unnecessary bootstrap steps

evals.name: server-not-running-local-setup
evals.prompt: I just installed SkyPilot and want to try launching a GPU instance on AWS. I don't have a remote API server.
evals.expected_output: Agent runs `sky api status -l 1`, detects 'SkyPilot API server is not running.', asks about local vs remote, user indicates local, agent runs `sky api start`, re-checks with `sky api status -l 1`, then runs `sky check` for credentials since this is a fresh setup.

assertions.text: Agent runs `sky api status` as the first SkyPilot command
Agent asks the user whether they have a remote server or want to start locally
Agent runs `sky api start` after user confirms local setup
Agent re-runs `sky api status` after starting the server to confirm it's reachable
Agent runs `sky check` since this is a fresh setup

evals.name: server-not-running-remote-connect
evals.prompt: I need to connect to our team's SkyPilot API server at https://skypilot.internal.company.com and launch a fine-tuning job.
evals.expected_output: Agent runs `sky api status -l 1`, detects server not running, recognizes user already provided a remote URL, runs `sky api login -e https://skypilot.internal.company.com` without asking unnecessary questions, re-checks status, then proceeds to help with the fine-tuning job.

assertions.text: Agent runs `sky api status` as the first SkyPilot command
Agent uses `sky api login -e https://skypilot.internal.company.com` (not `sky api start`) since the user provided a remote endpoint
Agent does NOT ask the user for the server URL again since they already provided it
Agent re-runs `sky api status` after connecting to confirm it's reachable
